sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.64 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Say that a group$G$is an$n$-approximation\/ to the Nottingham group$J=N(p)$(as defined in Archive, 12.24) if$G$is an infinite pro-$p$group, and$G/ _n(G)$is isomorphic to$J/ _n(J)$. Does there exist a function$f(p)$such that, if$G$is an$f(p)$-approximation to the Nottingham group, then$_i(G)/ _{i+1}(G)$is isomorphic to$_i(J)/ _{i+1}(J)$for all$i$? Cf. 14.56. Note that an affirmative solution to thi...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
